g.data-narration: Welcome to Descriptive Statistics in Machine Learning - the foundation of data analysis.
Descriptive statistics summarize and organize data to reveal basic characteristics before applying ML algorithms.
g.text: Tools to summarize, organize, and simplify
Foundation of data analysis in ML

g.data-narration: There are three main types: Central Tendency, Dispersion, and Shape measures - each serving unique purposes.
Measures of Central Tendency describe the center point - the typical value that represents the entire dataset.
g.text: Describe the "center" or typical value of a dataset

g.data-narration: The Mean is the arithmetic average - sum all values divided by count. Best for normal distributions but sensitive to outliers.
g.text: (Sum of all values ÷ Number of values)

g.data-narration: The Median is the middle value when sorted - divide data into two equal halves. Resistant to outliers.
g.text: Odd n: Median = value at position (n+1)/2
Even n: Median = avg of positions n/2 and n/2+1

g.data-narration: The Mode is the most frequently occurring value. Perfect for categorical data but may not exist or be unique.
Choose Mean for normal data, Median for skewed/outlier data, Mode for categorical data. Each has specific use cases.
Measures of Dispersion show how spread out data values are - revealing data consistency and variability.
g.text: Describe how spread out the data values are

g.data-narration: Range is max-min, Variance is average squared deviation from mean, SD is square root of variance - key ML metrics.
IQR is Q3-Q1, representing middle 50% of data. Used for outlier detection: below Q1-1.5×IQR or above Q3+1.5×IQR.
Skewness measures asymmetry - positive means right tail, negative means left tail. Kurtosis measures tail heaviness.
Histograms display data distribution by dividing into bins - showing frequency patterns and distribution shape visually.
g.text: Shows frequency distribution of numerical data

g.data-narration: Box plots show five-number summary. Scatter plots reveal relationships between two variables - essential for bivariate analysis.
In ML, descriptive statistics enable feature selection via variance thresholding, preprocessing for outliers, and model evaluation.
g.text: • Feature Selection: Variance thresholding, Correlation analysis
• Preprocessing: Handle missing values, Detect/treat outliers
• Model Evaluation: Residual distributions, Error patterns

g.data-narration: Key formulas to remember: Mean = Σx/n, Variance = Σ(x-μ)²/N, SD = √Variance, IQR = Q3-Q1.
g.text: Remember: Population uses N, Sample uses (n-1)

g.data-narration: For exams: Practice manual calculations, know when to use each measure, understand population vs sample formulas, read visualizations.
g.text: 1. Mean for normal data, Median for skewed/outliers, Mode for categorical
2. Practice calculating mean, variance, SD manually - common exam questions
3. Know population (N) vs sample (n-1) formula differences
4. IQR method for outliers: below Q1-1.5×IQR or above Q3+1.5×IQR
